Identity.History: Shapur II ruled the Sasanian Empire from 309–379 AD, one of the longest reigns in world history. Crowned while still an infant, he later became a powerful warrior-king who restored Sasanian strength after years of instability. He campaigned against Arab tribes, Huns, Armenians, and Rome, securing Persia’s frontiers and winning major victories in Mesopotamia. His long wars with Rome ended with Persia gaining influence over Armenia and border territories. Shapur strengthened royal authority, promoted Zoroastrianism, and left the empire militarily stronger.
